perturbation.original_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created. Turpin hereby irrevocably and expressly waives as against any person all moral rights Turpin may have in any and all Work Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Turpin acknowledges and agrees that while Work Product is generally made for the Company, specific exceptions may exist and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein unless otherwise agreed in writing. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created. Turpin hereby irrevocably and expressly waives as against any person all moral rights Turpin may have in any and all Work Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all Work Product is made for and owned exclusively by the company. The modified text introduces an exception, stating that this ownership applies 'unless otherwise agreed in writing.' This contradicts the initial blanket statement of company ownership, creating uncertainty about when and how Turpin could retain rights to Work Product. This exception clause affects section 5 where it states Turpin cannot assist any third party contesting Company's right on the Work Product.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Protection of Work Product. Turpin covenants that Turpin shall not at any time directly or indirectly contest or assist any third party in contesting the Company's right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product or any Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin shall not, directly or indirectly, apply for or seek registration of any Intellectual Property Rights in any Work Product in any jurisdiction without the express written approval of the Company. Turpin waives any and all existing and future moral rights with respect to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all Work Product is exclusively owned by the company. The changed text introduces an exception ('unless otherwise agreed in writing'). This directly contradicts the 'Protection of Work Product' section, where Turpin cannot contest the Company's right to the Work Product. The introduction of an exception means that under certain agreements, Turpin could retain rights, which would allow them to contest the Company's right, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Turpin shall deliver to the Company all Work Product and Company Property, including all originals and copies thereof, in Turpin's possession and/or control, at the request of the Company, or, in the absence of such a request, upon the termination of Turpin's employment with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Turpin shall deliver to the Company all Work Product and Company Property, including all originals and copies thereof, in Turpin's possession and/or control, at the request of the Company; however, certain proprietary information developed by Turpin prior to the agreement but utilized during employment may be retained by Turpin for personal archival purposes, provided it doesn't directly compete with the Company’s business, or, in the absence of such a request, upon the termination of Turpin's employment with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return of all Work Product and Company Property upon request or termination. The modified text introduces an exception allowing Turpin to retain certain 'proprietary information developed by Turpin prior to the agreement' under specific conditions. This contradicts the original 'all-encompassing' requirement for the delivery of all work product, making it ambiguous what must actually be returned. The term is contradicted in section 2, where it speaks about ownership of the company, the introduction of this exception clause about the proprietary information can create ambiguity and uncertainty. The clause added on location 6 directly impacts location 2, where it creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership by the Company. Turpin acknowledges and agrees that all Work Product have been made for the Company and that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein. Turpin does hereby assign and transfer to the Company, effective upon creation, all right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to the Work Product and all Intellectual Property Rights therein and does hereby assign all of Turpin's future right, title, and interest that Turpin may have in and to each of the Work Product and Intellectual Property Rights therein, effective at the time each is created. Turpin hereby irrevocably and expressly waives as against any person all moral rights Turpin may have in any and all Work Product.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception allowing Turpin to retain certain 'proprietary information developed by Turpin prior to the agreement,' which directly contradicts the original and the contradicted text's all-encompassing requirement that the Company shall be the exclusive owner of all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding what constitutes 'Work Product' and what must be returned, making it unclear what rights the company actually has, according to the contract. The exception directly conflicts with the Company's ownership as stated in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's employment with the Company terminate for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may, with written notice, be terminated by either party should Turpin's employment with the Company terminate for any reason, except in cases where confidential information was shared between the two parties, where the agreement will remain in effect unless specified otherwise in the termination agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement may be terminated by either party if Turpin's employment ends 'for any reason'. The modified text adds an exception: if confidential information was shared, the agreement remains in effect unless the termination agreement specifies otherwise. This creates a contradiction: while termination is generally allowed, it's now restricted under certain circumstances. This conflicts with Section 10, where the agreement is binding upon the company and its successors, but with that termination clause, it is now uncertain about that section due to this contradictory statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Successors and Assigns. This Agreement shall be binding upon Turpin and the heirs and legal representatives of Turpin, and shall be binding upon and ensure to the benefit of the Company and its successors and assigns, including any corporation with which or into which the Company or its successors may be merged or which may succeed, to its assets or business.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed termination 'for any reason.' The changed text creates an exception where termination is not allowed if confidential information was shared, unless the termination agreement specifies otherwise. This contradicts the broad, unconditional termination right previously granted. This conflicts with the clause stating the agreement is binding upon the company and its successors, because the added exception creates uncertainty about the continuation of the agreement in certain circumstances related to confidential information, thereby weakening or negating the original binding nature outlined in 'Successors and Assigns'.
